title: aconitum jeholense var. jeholense
intro: Aconitum jeholense var. jeholense, also known as Jehol Monkshood, is a perennial plant belonging to the Aconitum genus, characterized by its blue-violet flowers and tall, erect stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which can be attributed to the presence of aconitine, a toxic alkaloid. Aconitum jeholense var. jeholense has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, and has been used to treat conditions such as arthritis and sciatica. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used in decoctions, infusions, and ointments, although caution is advised due to its toxicity and potential side effects.
